title: eritrichium pulvinatum
intro: Eritrichium pulvinatum, also known as the purple filaree or globe alfalfa, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to the western United States and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, thanks to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. Eritrichium pulvinatum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for easy integration into herbal remedies and promoting overall well-being.
